title: tsuga diversifolia subsp. blaringhemii
intro: Tsuga diversifolia subsp. blaringhemii is a subspecies of coniferous evergreen tree, also known as Japanese hemlock, that is native to Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The leaves of the Tsuga diversifolia subsp. blaringhemii plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
